mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer confidence and disposable income, driving equity valuations higher over the subsequent 20 trading days through elevated earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135354.0 thousand as of Jan 2012
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeds 150k persons monthly for 3+ consecutive months yet SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of such instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains below 1.0% in a low-rate regime, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces the cost of capital and increases the present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.24% as of Jan 2012 (note: this appears to be a data error; actual Feb 2012 FEDFUNDS was ~0.14%)
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,800 thousand units combined with retail sales growth (RSAFS) signals broad-based demand expansion across residential and consumer sectors, triggering a joint multiplier effect that elevates equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1850.0 thousand as of Dec 2011
fact:RSAFS at 367941.0 million as of Jan 2012
failure_condition: Both HOUST and RSAFS conditions are met simultaneously in >40 instances over a 3-year window, yet SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of those instances, or joint Rank IC < 0.06.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5% signals a contraction in real economic capacity utilization, which compresses profit margins and reduces forward earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines >0.5% month-over-month in >30 instances over 3 years, yet SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of those instances, or Rank IC > -0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which depresses equity valuations through lower expected corporate revenues and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: UNRATE exceeds 5.0% in >50 monthly observations over 3 years, yet SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in >55% of those periods, or Rank IC > -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) signals monetary tightening pressure that raises real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Both inflation and funds rate conditions are met in >35 instances over 3 years, yet SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of those instances, or joint Rank IC > -0.06.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity multiples contract.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11385.3 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q3 2011
failure_condition: GDPC1 annualized growth falls below 2.0% in >25 quarterly observations over 3 years, yet SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of those periods, or Rank IC > -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) accelerate month-over-month by more than 1.0% signals robust consumer demand and forward earnings momentum across discretionary sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through immediate sentiment and technical momentum effects.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 367941.0 million as of Jan 2012
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 1.0% in >40 instances over 3 years, yet SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of those instances, or Rank IC < 0.05.
